overview: Bheema Institute of Technology and Science (BITS), established in 2008, is a private engineering college located in Adoni, Kurnool district, Andhra Pradesh. The institution is dedicated to providing technical education to students in the rural and semi-urban regions of Rayalaseema, focusing on engineering and technology disciplines.
accreditation: Approved by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), New Delhi. Affiliated with Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University, Anantapur (JNTUA).

coursesAndFees.course: B.Tech (CSE, AI&ML, ECE, EEE, Civil, Mechanical)
coursesAndFees.eligibility: 10+2 with 45% aggregate in PCM + AP EAPCET rank

coursesAndFees.course: M.Tech (CSE, Embedded Systems, Power Systems, Highway Engg, etc.)

admissions.admissionProcess: Admissions are primarily through state-level counseling (APSCHE). 70% of seats are filled under the Convener Quota (Category A) based on entrance exam ranks, and 30% are Management Quota (Category B) seats filled based on merit.
admissions.importantDates: AP EAPCET 2026 Engineering Exam: May 12 - May 18, 2026; AP PGECET 2026: May 2026; AP POLYCET 2026: April-May 2026.

facilities: Separate Hostels for Boys and Girls
campus.specialNote: The college is particularly noted for its support of students from economically weaker backgrounds and its functional laboratory facilities that emphasize practical learning.

contact.address: Alur Road, Mandigiri (V), Adoni, Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh - 518301
